arabic_text:
مولانا محمد بن اسماعیل (7)
محتجب (قائمت کی ہیکل)
حجاب (امامت کی ہیکل)
یعلی بن محمد بن اسماعیل (8)
عبد اللہ بن محمد بن اسماعیل (8)
ابو طالب بن یعلی (9)
احمد بن عبد اللہ (9)
علی بن ابو طالب (10)
حسین بن احمد (10)
عبد اللہ (المہدی باللہ) بن حسین (11)
قائم الحاکم بأمر اللہ (12)
english_text:
Our Master Muhammad bin Ismail (7)
Veiled (Form of Qa'imiyat)
Veil (Form of Imamate)
Ya'la bin Muhammad bin Ismail (8)
Abdullah bin Muhammad bin Ismail (8)
Abu Talib bin Ya'la (9)
Ahmad bin Abdullah (9)
Ali bin Abu Talib (10)
Husayn bin Ahmad (10)
Abdullah (al-Mahdi bi-Llah) bin Husayn (11)
Qa'im al-Hakim bi-Amr Allah (12)
alt_text: A two-column genealogical table starting from 'مولانا محمد بن اسماعیل (7)' (Our Master Muhammad bin Ismail, generation 7) at the top. The columns are: left 'حجاب (امامت کی ہیکل)' (Veil — Form of Imamate, visible line), right 'محتجب (قائمت کی ہیکل)' (Veiled — Form of Qa'imiyat, concealed line). Rows by generation: 8 — Abdullah bin Muhammad bin Ismail | Ya'la bin Muhammad bin Ismail. 9 — Ahmad bin Abdullah | Abu Talib bin Ya'la. 10 — Husayn bin Ahmad | Ali bin Abu Talib. 11 — Abdullah (al-Mahdi bi-Llah) bin Husayn (visible line only). 12 — Qa'im al-Hakim bi-Amr Allah (concealed line only).
notes: Historical Fatimid succession diagram from Muhammad bin Ismail (the seventh Ismaili Imam) down through the dawr al-satr (Concealment Cycle) to the founding Fatimid caliphs: al-Mahdi bi-Llah and al-Qa'im bi-Amr Allah. The two parallel lines (Veil/visible vs Veiled/concealed) capture the doctrinal model where each generation had both a manifest and a hidden Imamic figure during the period of concealment.
